name: Fable of the Mirror-Breaker // Reflection of Kiki-Jiki
type_line: Enchantment — Saga // Enchantment Creature — Goblin Shaman
oracle_text:
(As this Saga enters and after your draw step, add a lore counter. After III, exile this, then return it to the battlefield transformed.)
I — Create a 2/2 red Goblin Shaman creature token with "Whenever this creature attacks, create a Treasure token."
II — You may discard up to two cards. If you do, draw that many cards.
III — Exile this Saga, then return it to the battlefield transformed.

card_faces.name: Fable of the Mirror-Breaker
card_faces.oracle_text:
(As this Saga enters and after your draw step, add a lore counter. After III, exile this, then return it to the battlefield transformed.)
I — Create a 2/2 red Goblin Shaman creature token with "Whenever this creature attacks, create a Treasure token."
II — You may discard up to two cards. If you do, draw that many cards.
III — Exile this Saga, then return it to the battlefield transformed.

card_faces.name: Reflection of Kiki-Jiki
card_faces.oracle_text: {1}, {T}: Create a token that's a copy of another nonlegendary creature you control, except it has haste. Sacrifice it at the beginning of the next end step.

design_notes: A transform Saga — one of the most complex card types in modern MTG. Demonstrates: (1) double-faced cards with // in the name, (2) Saga chapter abilities formatted as Roman numeral — effect, (3) transform layout uses card_faces array, (4) the back face is a completely different card type (Enchantment Creature). Each chapter provides distinct value (token, card filtering, transformation), showing how Sagas tell a story through mechanics.
